name: Non-Sustained VT (NSVT)
identifyBy: ≥3 consecutive ventricular beats at rate >100 bpm
Wide bizarre QRS complexes - ventricular morphology
Often asymptomatic or causes brief palpitations/dizziness
management: Isolated asymptomatic NSVT in structurally normal heart: often benign - monitor
NSVT with structural heart disease (cardiomyopathy, post-MI): significant risk marker - cardiology referral
Correct reversible triggers: electrolytes, ischaemia, drugs
Symptomatic or frequent NSVT: Holter monitoring, echocardiogram, cardiology review
ICD evaluation if NSVT + EF <35% (MADIT-2 criteria)
pearls: NSVT in a healthy young person during exercise: often benign (outflow tract VT)
NSVT in post-MI patient or cardiomyopathy: significant risk of sustained VT/sudden death
Torsades de Pointes may present as recurrent NSVT - check QT interval in sinus beats
Right ventricular outflow tract (RVOT) VT: LBBB morphology with inferior axis - typically benign
Holter monitoring: captures frequency, rate, and duration of NSVT runs

qaNotes: Regular broad-complex ventricular tachycardia with AV dissociation/absent visible P waves.
